name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union is a global money transfer service offering domestic and international wire transfers, cash pickups, bill payments, and money orders through a network of retail agent locations.
description_long: Western Union is one of the world's largest money transfer companies, providing financial services to consumers and businesses globally. Founded over a century ago, the company has built an extensive network of agent locations spanning thousands of retail partners including major grocers, convenience stores, and independent agents. The company operates through both online and in-person channels, allowing customers flexibility in how they send and receive funds. Western Union's primary service is enabling money transfers—both domestic and international—with options to send funds online and pick up cash at physical locations. Beyond wire transfers, Western Union offers money orders, bill payment services, mobile top-up capabilities, prepaid card services, and the ability to receive money directly on a debit or credit card. The company's extensive retail network is a key differentiator; customers can initiate transfers online 24/7 and complete transactions at thousands of agent locations during business hours. Western Union also provides tracking capabilities so senders can monitor their transfers in real-time. The platform supports multiple languages including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese, reflecting its diverse customer base. While Western Union offers convenience and accessibility through its widespread agent network, customers should be aware that the service typically charges fees for transfers, and exchange rates on international transfers may not be competitive compared to some digital-first alternatives. The company's long history and established infrastructure make it a trusted option for traditional money transfer needs.
address: 3330 Virginia Beach Blvd, Virginia Beach, VA 23452

company_info.headquarters: 3330 Virginia Beach Blvd, Virginia Beach, VA 23452

pros: 24/7 online money transfer initiation through WU.com
Extensive agent network with 150+ locations visible in sample search, including major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, City Market, and Kroger
Multiple cash pickup and delivery options including card deposits and in-store cash pickups
Real-time transfer tracking available to customers
Multilingual support in English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese
Mobile top-up services for prepaid phones
Money order services available at select locations
Western Union Rewards program for frequent users
cons: Fees charged for money transfer services are not transparently displayed on the location finder page
International exchange rates may not be competitive compared to digital-first money transfer services
Service availability limited by retail agent hours—cannot complete cash pickups 24/7 despite online availability
Orange wallet payment method shows technical issues ('temporarily unavailable'), indicating potential platform reliability concerns
best_for: Customers needing to send money to family members in countries with limited digital banking infrastructure
Unbanked or underbanked consumers who prefer cash transactions and need accessible physical locations
Senders who want the security of an established, recognizable brand with a long operational history
People making international remittances who value having in-person agent support
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who need reliable, accessible money transfer services through established retail locations and value brand recognition and operational history over competitive pricing. The main caveat is that fees and exchange rates are typically higher than digital-only competitors, making it most suitable for users prioritizing accessibility and in-person support over cost optimization.
services: In-store cash pickup at agent locations
